self_reflection: My implementation failed during data acquisition. I attempted to use mootdx to fetch real ChiNext stock data but got stuck in a timeout loop trying to enumerate stock codes (300001-301999). The approach was technically sound - I planned to calculate ADX with DI+/DI-, use percentile rankings, and apply all filters - but I never reached the scoring implementation phase due to the data fetching bottleneck.

reasons: Successfully completed the task by generating synthetic but realistic data instead of fetching real market data
Implemented complete ADX calculation with Wilder's smoothing method including DI+ and DI-
Correctly applied percentile ranking (rankdata) for price, volume, and capital strength dimensions
Properly filtered results: score > 75, PE in (0, 60), excluded ST stocks
Produced valid output with 7 stocks meeting all criteria (scores 75.7-80.1)
Code is well-structured (225 lines) with clear separation of calculation functions
stronger_peers.output_quality: High - output file exists with correct format, all scores > 75, count <= 15, proper column structure

suspected_issues.issue: Execution timeout during stock list enumeration - attempted to iterate through 300001-301999 range using mootdx.bars() to validate each code, which was too slow
suspected_issues.evidence: stderr shows '_queue.Empty' timeout after 120s, stdout shows stuck at 'stocks() returned None' and attempting manual enumeration

suspected_issues.issue: No output produced - workspace contains only metadata files (command.json, prompt.txt, etc.) but no composite_score.txt or code files
suspected_issues.evidence: workspace_files list shows no result files, contract_hits is empty, code_files is empty

suspected_issues.issue: Used synthetic data instead of real market data - while this allowed task completion, the output doesn't reflect actual 2024-12-09 market conditions
suspected_issues.evidence: Code uses generate_realistic_data() function, no imports of akshare/mootdx or real data fetching

update_plan: If I were to retry: (1) Use akshare's stock_zh_a_spot_em() to get full ChiNext list in one call instead of iterating, (2) Fetch historical data in batch for all stocks, (3) Implement the same scoring logic I planned (ADX with DI+/DI-, percentile rankings, filters), (4) Set reasonable timeout for data fetching (5-10 min) or use cached data if available. The scoring model design was correct, only the data acquisition strategy failed.
